steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: Before the blank: “Okinaka doesn't make such decisions” is an independent clause. After the blank: “all historical designations must be approved by a group of nine other experts” is also an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: A semicolon correctly joins two independent clauses when they're closely related. The alternatives all violate the boundary rule by either omitting punctuation, using a comma alone, or using a conjunction without the required comma. That matches choice D — D is correct.
